title: quercus fruticosa var. subinclusa
intro: Quercus fruticosa var. subinclusa, also known as the Korean white oak or Korean scrub oak, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Korea and China. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antimicrobial agent. Quercus fruticosa var. subinclusa can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
